subject: Cloud Computing
title: Reserved Instances and Commitments
description: Learn Reserved Instances and Commitments in one minute.

hook.text: AWS offers a deal: commit to one year and save 40%. Commit to three years and save 60%. Tempting.

buildup.text: Reserved instances trade flexibility for savings. You promise to use a specific amount, and the price drops.

discovery.text: All major clouds offer this: AWS Reserved Instances, Azure Reservations, GCP Committed Use Discounts.

twist.text: Commit to the wrong size or region and you're stuck paying for resources you don't need for years.

climax.text: Reservations save large companies millions per year. But they require accurate forecasting — a rare skill.

punchline.text: Commit and save big. Guess wrong and waste big.

quiz.question: What is the trade-off of reserved instances?
options: Lower prices in exchange for a commitment to use specific resources over time
Higher prices in exchange for more technical support
Free resources in exchange for sharing your data publicly
